system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (5 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (5 0 0);
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {

    }

    top

    }

    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet

        type            fixedValue;

    }

    outlet

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;

    top

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;

    {

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;

        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;

        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {

    }

    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - lower left (below wedge)
    (-0.15242  0      -0.005)   // 0
    ( 0          0      -0.005)   // 1
    ( 0          0.1524  -0.005)   // 2
    (-0.15242  0.1524  -0.005)   // 3

    // Block 1 - upper right (above wedge)
    ( 0          0      0.005)    // 4
    ( 0.3048      0      0.005)    // 5
    ( 0.3048      0.1524  0.005)    // 6
    (-0.15242  0.1524  0.005)    // 7
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Rectangular region below wedge
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (30 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Rectangular region above wedge
    hex (0 3 7 4 1 2 6 5) (60 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (2 3 7 6)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
            (7 6 5 2)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
            (4 0 1 5)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Trying to Specify a Boundary Face Already Belongs to Another Patch

**Error Message:**
```
Trying to specify a boundary face 4(0 1 5 4) on the face on cell 0 which is either an internal face or already belongs to some other patch. This is face 0 of patch 0 named inlet.
```

This is a **fundamental topology conflict**. The error occurs because the face `(0 1 5 4)` is being assigned to the `inlet` patch, but this same face is also part of another patch (specifically, it's also listed under `frontAndBack`). 

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, the `frontAndBack` patch includes the face `(1 2 6 5)`, but the `inlet` patch includes `(0 1 5 4)`. These are different faces, so why is there a conflict?

The issue is that **Block 0** contains the face `(0 1 5 4)` as one of its internal faces. In a hex block `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`, the six faces are:
- Bottom: `(0 1 2 3)`
- Top: `(4 5 6 7)`
- Front: `(0 1 5 4)`
- Back: `(3 2 6 7)`
- Left: `(0 3 7 4)`
- Right: `(1 2 6 5)`

The `inlet` patch is defined as `(0 1 5 4)` — this is the **Front** face of Block 0. The `frontAndBack` patch is defined as `(0 3 7 4)` and `(1 2 6 5)` — these are the **Left** and **Right** faces of Block 0. So far, no overlap.

However, the `outlet` patch is defined as `(2 3 7 6)`. This is the **Back** face of Block 0 combined with part of Block 1. The `top` patch includes `(3 7 6 2)` and `(7 6 5 2)` — the **Top** face of Block 0 and parts of Block 1. The `bottom` patch includes `(0 4 5 1)` and `(4 0 1 5)` — the **Bottom** face of Block 0 and parts of Block 1.

The conflict arises because **multiple boundary patches are trying to claim the same face**. Specifically, the face `(0 1 5 4)` (Front face of Block 0) is listed in the `inlet` patch, but it may also be implicitly included in another patch definition due to how the face lists are structured.

Looking more carefully at the `frontAndBack` patch definition:
```
frontAndBack
{
    type patch;
    faces
    (
        (0 3 7 4)
        (1 2 6 5)
    );
}
```

This assigns faces `(0 3 7 4)` and `(1 2 6 5)` to `frontAndBack`. These are the Left and Right faces of Block 0. The `inlet` patch has `(0 1 5 4)` — the Front face. The `outlet` patch has `(2 3 7 6)` — which combines part of Block 0's Back face `(3 2 6 7)` with Block 1's geometry. The `top` patch has `(3 7 6 2)` and `(7 6 5 2)` — combining Block 0's Top face `(4 5 6 7)` with Block 1's geometry. The `bottom` patch has `(0 4 5 1)` and `(4 0 1 5)` — combining Block 0's Bottom face `(0 1 5 4)` with Block 1's geometry.

**The root problem is that the face `(0 1 5 4)` (Front face of Block 0) is being claimed by TWO different boundary patches**: `inlet` and indirectly by `bottom` (through the face `(0 4 5 1)` which is the same quadrilateral but with vertices reordered). In OpenFOAM, a face cannot belong to multiple patches simultaneously.

### Proposed Fix:

The `bottom` patch should NOT include the face `(0 4 5 1)`. The bottom face of Block 0 is `(0 1 5 4)` — this is the **inlet** face, not the bottom face. The true bottom face of the entire domain is the face at z = -0.005, which consists of:
- Block 0: `(0 1 2 3)` — the bottom face of Block 0
- Block 1: `(4 5 6 7)` — the bottom face of Block 1

So the `bottom` patch should be defined as:
```
bottom
{
    type patch;
    faces
    (
        (0 1 2 3)
        (4 5 6 7)
    );
}
```

And the `inlet` patch should be defined as:
```
inlet
{
    type patch;
    faces
    (
        (0 1 5 4)
    );
}
```

The `frontAndBack` patch should remain as:
```
frontAndBack
{
    type patch;
    faces
    (
        (0 3 7 4)
        (1 2 6 5)
    );
}
```

The `outlet` patch should combine the back faces of both blocks:
```
outlet
{
    type patch;
    faces
    (
        (2 3 7 6)
    );
}
```

The `top` patch should combine the top faces of both blocks:
```
top
{
    type patch;
    faces
    (
        (3 7 6 2)
        (7 6 5 2)
    );
}
```

The `wedge` patch should remain as the shared slanted face:
```
wedge
{
    type wall;
    faces
    (
        (1 2 6 5)
    );
}
```

This reorganization ensures that each face of every block is assigned to exactly one boundary patch, eliminating the duplication/conflict.

## Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cannot Find Points File

This is purely a cascading error — once `blockMesh` succeeds, the mesh will exist and `rhoCentralFoam` will work. No action needed here beyond fixing `blockMesh`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change bottom patch face list from (0 4 5 1) and (4 0 1 5) to (0 1 2 3) and (4 5 6 7) to assign the actual bottom faces of Block 0 and Block 1 respectively; Remove face (0 1 5 4) from bottom patch since that is the inlet face; Keep inlet patch as (0 1 5 4); Keep frontAndBack as (0 3 7 4) and (1 2 6 5); Keep outlet as (2 3 7 6); Keep top as (3 7 6 2) and (7 6 5 2); Keep wedge as (1 2 6 5)'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 5 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
